doom.driver: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options. Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.
doom.blurb: CENTCOM HAS A PLAN. OIL HITS $126.
doom.summary: The US-Iran war just crossed day 62 with no end date, a $25 billion price tag, and now CENTCOM drafting fresh strike options while Brent crude briefly kissed $126 a barrel — the highest since 2022. Trump is simultaneously threatening to pull troops from Germany, leaving NATO allies to wonder if America is breaking up with its friends mid-war. The Strait of Hormuz remains choked, Iran's shadow fleet is running dark-ship ops to dodge the blockade, and wildfire season hasn't even started yet — which will now cost tens of millions more because jet fuel has nearly doubled.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: CENTCOM strike options being prepared for Trump briefing while oil hits $126, Iran blockade holds at day 62 with no exit ramp visible.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump threatens Germany troop cuts mid-war, Supreme Court weakens Voting Rights Act, and Comey faces charges over a seashell Instagram post.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: OpenAI's restricted GPT-5.5-Cyber model rolls out to 'critical cyber defenders only' — a frontier cybersecurity AI with controlled access is still a frontier cybersecurity AI.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: AI coding agent Cursor deleted an entire production database in 9 seconds; seven families sue OpenAI alleging ChatGPT activity flagged a mass shooter that the company didn't report.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Drone strikes on Middle East data centers have spooked Big Tech into halting regional projects — war is now literally destroying cloud infrastructure.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, extreme close-up filling the entire frame with the face of a single steel crude oil barrel viewed dead-on, its dented rusted-red surface dominating every pixel — the barrel stamped with bold stenciled pricing digits barely legible through corrosion, the metal surface reflecting a distorted panorama of the world trapped in its curved skin: in the dark convex reflection, the silhouette of an immense VLCC supertanker sits low and heavy on hammered-bronze Persian Gulf water, flanked by the angular gray profile of a guided-missile destroyer with VLS cells visible on its forward deck, the Iranian coastline a haze-blurred ridge on the horizon with orange-yellow flare stacks burning against a pre-dawn sky above Bandar Abbas, heat shimmer already rising from the flat-calm water — all of this compressed and curved into the barrel's belly like a terrible omen — the barrel itself lit from below by deep firelight, casting dark crimson gradients across pitted steel, charcoal shadow pooling in every dent and seam, rust streaks running like dried blood down the drum's face, the overall palette a suffocating dark crimson and charcoal with high contrast firelight catching the barrel's upper rim in a thin burning orange line, no antialiasing, no text, 16-bit pixel clusters rendering every rust pit and reflection in brutal chunky detail.

dawn.driver: 'Historic breakthrough': Colombia climate talks end with hopes raised for fossil fuel phaseout. Nearly 60 countries back voluntary roadmaps to wean world off coal, oil and gas, at conference prompted by frustration with UN climate summits
dawn.blurb: NEARLY 60 NATIONS AGREE TO QUIT FOSSIL FUELS
dawn.summary: While the Iran war grinds toward a $25 billion price tag and oil hits highs not seen since 2022, nearly 60 nations gathered in Santa Marta, Colombia and quietly did something remarkable: agreed on voluntary roadmaps to phase out coal, oil, and gas. The summit was born from frustration with sluggish UN processes — meaning this represents countries deciding to move faster, not slower, than the system allows. It won't fix everything overnight, but when the world is literally burning through fossil fuels to fund a war, watching dozens of governments commit in the other direction is the kind of signal that compounds.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Nearly 60 countries endorsing fossil fuel phaseout roadmaps at Santa Marta is the broadest voluntary climate coalition assembled outside a formal UN process.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: The summit was driven by frustration with gridlocked multilateral bodies — meaning nations self-organized around ambition rather than waiting for consensus from the slowest actors.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Scientists successfully teleported a photon's quantum state across a 270-meter open-air link between two separate quantum dots, a landmark in quantum networking.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Thousands of US hockey fans in Buffalo spontaneously sang the Canadian national anthem when a microphone cut out, a small act of cross-border solidarity amid political tensions.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: The Artemis II crew appeared on GMA and at the White House after completing the farthest human journey from Earth in over 50 years — a reminder that our reach still extends beyond this planet's conflicts.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Silhouette composition, strong backlight. The Caribbean coastline of Santa Marta at golden dusk, Sierra Nevada mountains rising in deep indigo silhouette against a blazing sky of molten gold and emerald green, the sea glittering amber and jade below. In the foreground, two women stand side by side in powerful silhouette on an open terrace or balcony overlooking the coast, their postures upright and resolute, one gesturing outward toward the horizon. Behind them through tall open doors, the warm interior glow of a conference hall spills outward, and the faint silhouettes of dozens of national flags line the table within, their poles creating a dense forest of tiny shapes. To one side, a large unrolled document or map is spread flat on a table, backlit so its edges glow gold, ink fresh and luminous. A small cluster of silhouetted figures stands nearby in quiet deliberation. Far out on the horizon, the faint low flat outline of a tiny island nation sits just above the waterline, barely visible, fragile against the vast glowing sea. The entire scene is saturated in rich gold, deep emerald, and warm amber, tropical light flooding every surface, dawn-like in its tender triumphant glow.
